channel.title: SOC Intelligence Digest
channel.description: Seven-day operational threat intelligence and IOC reporting for SOC review. Coverage window: 2026/06/18 23:54 - 2026/06/25 23:54 JST. Article dates: 2026/06/19 - 2026/06/25 JST.

item.title: STOCKSTAY Another Day: The Latest Addition to Turla’s Intelligence Gathering Apparatus
item.description: Source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, APT, Threat Actor, Cloud, Endpoint, Network, Cryptocurrency, Application Security / Topics: Turla, Microsoft, Malware, APT, Cloud / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (93) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN critical sector, JA attack activity, IOC 43, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector / Summary: Written by: Jordan Jones Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has conducted an in-depth analysis of a .NET backdoor, tracked as STOCKSTAY, that has been continually developed and deployed by the Russia-linked threat actor Turla (aka SUMMIT, Secret Blizzard, VENOMOUS BEAR, UAC-0194) since at least December 2022. Turla has deployed STOCKSTAY against government and military organizations in Ukraine, as well as entities with an interest in Italian foreign policy. Used for ongoing cyber espionage, this backdoor shares significant code and functional overlaps with KAZUAR, a successfu

item.title: Zero-Day Exploitation of Vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager
item.description: Source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Threat Actor, Cloud, Identity, AI Security, Network, Detection, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: CVE-2026-20245, Cisco, Web Application, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Cloud, Identity, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (93) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, IOC 15, coherent attack event, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector / Summary: Written by: Chester Sng, Pete Boonyakarn, Logeswaran Nadarajan Introduction In early 2026, Mandiant identified a threat actor targeting SD-WAN infrastructure at a service provider. After gaining initial access, the threat actor exploited a zero-day vulnerability ( CVE-2026-20245 ) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN to escalate privileges from a compromised administrative account to root-level access. The vulnerability stems from the device’s file upload feature lacking the ability to properly filter malicious data. Throughout the intrusion, to maintain operational security and avoid detection, the threa / Also reported by: Bleeping Computer, The HackersNews, Security Affairs

item.title: CVE-2025-54068 Laravel Livewire Credential Theft Campaign: 6,000+ Applications Compromised
item.description: Source: Imperva / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Application Security, Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, Identity / Topics: CVE-2025-54068, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, Identity / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, IOC 5, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context / Summary: Introduction On May 24, 2026, Imperva observed exploitation attempts against Laravel Livewire applications, blocked by the Imperva Cloud WAF. What initially appeared to be unremarkable deserialization attack traffic turned out to be part of a large-scale credential theft operation exploiting CVE-2025-54068, a critical unauthenticated RCE vulnerability in Laravel Livewire v3 (versions up to v3.6.3). The […] The post CVE-2025-54068 Laravel Livewire Credential Theft Campaign: 6,000+ Applications Compromised appeared first on Blog .

item.title: Snake Keylogger（マルウェア図鑑）
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Exploit, Patch, Phishing, Threat Actor, Endpoint / Topics: Microsoft, Malware, Exploit, Phishing / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (81) / Reasons: EN attack activity, JA attack activity, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan source / Summary: 別名 404 Keylogger、Snake Stealer 活動開始時期 2020年ごろ プラットフォーム Windows カテゴリ キーロガー 概要 Snake Keyloggerは、主にMicrosoft Windowsを標的とする情報窃取型マルウェアです。 キーボード入力の記録（キーロギング）に加え、Webブラウザーやメールクライアントなどから認証情報を窃取する機能を持ちます。2020年前後から継続的に観測されており、フィッシングメールを利用した感染活動で広く拡散しました。 Snake Keyloggerは名前の通りキーロガーに分類されるマルウェアですが、さまざまな認証情報を窃取する情報窃取型マルウェアとしての機能も備えています。2020年代以降に登場したマルウェアは複数のカテゴリにまたがる機能を持つものが多く、Snake Keyloggerはその中でも代表的なマルウェアのうちの1つです。 また、Snake KeyloggerはMaaS（Malware-as-a-Service）として流通している点も特徴です。Snake Keylogger系のMaaSは月額ライセンス形式で提供され、アンダーグラウンドフォーラムやダークネットなどで販売されています。Builderや設定ツールなども含めて提供されることから、技術力の低い攻撃者でも比較的容易に利用できるとされています。さらに、多

item.title: What the Miasma campaign reveals about the new supply chain threat model and the underground market for developer credentials
item.description: Source: Tenable / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Exposure Management, Threat Intel, Exploit, Malware, APT, Threat Actor, Supply Chain, Cloud, Identity, ICS/OT, Data Breach, AI Security, Endpoint, Detection / Topics: Cloud Security, Microsoft, Operational Technology, Supply Chain, Exploit, Malware, APT, Cloud, Identity, ICS/OT, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context / Summary: A stolen session cookie sat in underground markets for seven weeks before attackers used it to poison 32 Red Hat packages in the npm software registry, an example of the industrial approach behind modern supply chain attacks. Key takeaways Miasma is a self-propagating npm worm derived from Mini Shai-Hulud that TeamPCP open-sourced on May 12. The public release of the full weaponized toolchain means any operator can now replicate structurally identical supply chain campaigns. The Miasma campaign compromised 89-plus npm packages across three waves (June 1-5), affecting Red Hat, Vapi.ai, and Micr

item.title: 生成AIを悪用した初のAndroidマルウェアPromptSpyの脅威
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, Threat Actor, Mobile, AI Security, Incident Response / Topics: Android, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Reasons: EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, JA attack activity, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, JA critical sector, IOC 31, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector / Summary: ESET社は生成AIを活用した初のAndroidマルウェアPromptSpyを発見しました。 AIによる動的な判定により、端末やUIの違いに依存せず動作し、被害対象を大幅に拡大させる可能性があります。その詳細について解説します。 本記事はESET Japanが提供する「ESETブログ」に掲載された「PromptSpy：生成AIを活用したAndroidを狙う新たな脅威の到来」を再編集したものです。 ESET社の研究者は、生成AIを悪用し、コンテキストに応じてユーザーインターフェースの操作を行うAndroidマルウェアの初の事例を発見しました。機械学習は、すでに同様の目的で使用されています。つい最近、Dr.WEBの研究者がTensorFlow機械学習モデルを用いて広告スクリーンショットを分析し、検出された要素を自動クリックして大規模な広告詐欺を行うAndroid.Phantomを発見しています。しかし、こうした形で生成AIが展開されたのは今回が初めてです。攻撃者は、悪意のあるUI操作を誘導するためにAIモデル（この例ではGoogleのGemini）のプロンプトに依存しているため、ESET社はこのマルウェア系統をPromptSpyと名付けました。AIを活用したマルウェアをESET社が発見した事例としては、2025年8月のPromptLockに続いて、これが2件目になります。 生成AIが展

item.title: KDDI Data Breach May Have Exposed Up to 14.22 Million Email Accounts
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Actor, Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Malware, Ransomware, Data Breach, Mobile, Network, Detection / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Vulnerability, Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (94) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: Medium (53) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN Japan/Asia relevance, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan related / Summary: Japanese telecommunications company KDDI has disclosed a major cybersecurity incident in which up to 14.22 million email addresses and passwords may have been exposed through systems used by multiple internet service providers. The KDDI data breach has now become one of the most recent security events involving shared ISP infrastructure in Japan. The company confirmed that the data breach at KDDI was detected on June 17, 2026, after unauthorized access was identified in an email system provided to ISP operators. KDDI said it immediately took steps to modify the affected system and deployed pro

item.title: CVE-2026-48907 and LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaws Come Under Active Attack
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Patch, Malware, Threat Actor, Network, Incident Response / Topics: CVE-2026-48907, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Malware / Priority: Critical (94) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: Medium (53) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, indicator context / Summary: Security researchers and software vendors warn that attackers are actively exploiting vulnerabilities in both Joomla and the LiteSpeed cPanel plugin, posing significant risks to website administrators and shared hosting environments. One of the most urgent issues is CVE-2026-48907, a critical vulnerability affecting the Joomla Content Editor (JCE). The flaw stems from an improper access-control weakness that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload editor profiles and, ultimately, execute arbitrary PHP code on vulnerable servers. Security experts say threat actors are already abusing the bug

item.title: Payouts King Ransomware Initial Access Broker Deploys New Edgecution Malware
item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Patch, Malware, Ransomware, Threat Actor, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: Microsoft, Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Critical (91) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (87) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, JA attack activity, IOC 2, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context / Summary: IntroductionZscaler ThreatLabz has been monitoring ransomware operations that align with tactics previously employed by an initial access broker affiliated with Payouts King ransomware. In recent attacks, the threat actor leverages social engineering tactics paired with an innovative malware delivery mechanism. The technique utilizes a malicious Microsoft Edge browser extension that exploits the Chrome native messaging protocol to interact with host-native applications beyond the confines of the browser sandbox. By abusing this interface, the attackers gain direct host access, enabling them to

item.title: VU#457458: Vendor-signed UEFI applications found vulnerable to Secure Boot bypass
item.description: Source: US CERT Vulnerability Notes / Source attributes: United States / CERT / Primary / Categories: Vulnerability, Exploit, Endpoint, Detection / Topics: Linux, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (91) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context / Summary: Overview Multiple vendor-signed UEFI applications are vulnerable to Secure Boot bypass via a "Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver" (BYOVD)-style attack. If a target system trusts the affected vendor’s certificate, an attacker can exploit these applications to execute arbitrary code during the early pre-boot phase before the operating system initializes. To mitigate this risk, system administrators should apply updates to the UEFI Forbidden Signature Database (DBX) that revoke trust in the affected vendor-signed binaries, preventing these vulnerable applications from executing during the boot proc

item.title: Top 10 Dark Web Markets in 2026: List & Threat Analysis
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Malware, Data Breach / Topics: Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (88) / SOC: 95 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, IOC 9, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: Top Dark Web Marketplaces in 2026 Dark Web marketplaces in 2026 are underground platforms where cybercriminals buy and sell stolen data, credentials, stealer logs, payment card records, malware, fraud services, counterfeit documents, drugs, and access to compromised systems. Also known as Dark Web markets or darknet markets, these platforms are increasingly specialized, with some operating […]

item.title: What Is FortiBleed? Your Questions Answered
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Threat Actor, Identity, Network / Topics: Fortinet, Network Edge, Identity / Priority: Critical (88) / SOC: 95 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, IOC 9, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context / Summary: What Is FortiBleed? Your Questions Answered This is a developing story. Figures and findings are updated as the investigation continues. 1. What is FortiBleed? FortiBleed is an active, large-scale credential theft campaign targeting internet-exposed Fortinet FortiGate firewalls and SSL VPN gateways. The same threat actor has also been observed targeting FortiWeb and MSSQL services as […]

item.title: 🏴‍☠️ Stormous has just published a new victim : mlit.com.my UPDATE-FULL DATA DUMP NEW LINK 10GB
item.description: Source: ransomware.live / Source attributes: France / Research / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intel, Network / Topics: Ransomware / Priority: Critical (83) / SOC: 70 / Confidence: High (75) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, target and impact context, Japan related, critical sector / Summary: FULL DATA DUMP . The compromised data includes highly sensitive internal operations and financial records. Among the leaked files are complete individual Campaign Profit and Loss (PnL) statements, detailed revenue sheets, clawbacks, and general ledger accounts for several linked entities, including Salesworks Pte Ltd Taiwan Branch and Shaves2u HK Limited. Additionally, we have extracted complete directory trees and file structures from the internal network shares and remote desktop sessions, revealing thousands of corporate folders such as JAG Group, SWGP Excel Import, and various financial da

item.title: 136 Critical Vulnerabilities, 212 Organizations, Zero Exploits Required: What Attackers See in Canadian Healthcare
item.description: Source: Flare Threat Intelligence / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Data Breach, Vulnerability, Ransomware, Identity / Topics: Ransomware, Data Breach, Vulnerability, Identity / Priority: Critical (81) / SOC: 79 / Confidence: High (81) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector / Summary: By Adrian Cheek, Senior Cybercrime Researcher In October 2023, a coordinated ransomware attack on five southwestern Ontario hospitals forced cancer treatments to be canceled, surgeries postponed, and emergency patients diverted to other facilities. Staff reverted to pen and paper. The attack compromised 5.6 million patient visits and cost roughly $7.5 million CAD in direct recovery. […] The post 136 Critical Vulnerabilities, 212 Organizations, Zero Exploits Required: What Attackers See in Canadian Healthcare appeared first on Flare | Identity First Threat Intelligence | Unmatched Visibility in

item.title: TfL Hackers Plead Guilty After Breach Exposed Customer Data and Cost £29 Million
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Ransomware, Threat Actor, Data Breach, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (80) / SOC: 89 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector / Summary: Two alleged members of the cybercrime collective Scattered Spider have pleaded guilty to their roles in the Transport for London cyberattack , an incident that disrupted services, exposed customer data, and resulted in approximately £29 million in losses and recovery costs for London's transport authority. The guilty pleas were entered by Thalha Jubair, 20, from East London, and Owen Flowers, 18, from Walsall, West Midlands, on the opening day of proceedings at Woolwich Crown Court. The pair had been due to stand trial on June 22 but changed their pleas to guilty. Transport for London Cyberatt

item.title: AIサービス「claude.ai」のチャット共有画面から不正なコマンドを実行させるClickFix型攻撃キャンペーンを分析
item.description: Source: Trend Micro / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, AI Security / Topics: Malware, AI Security / Priority: High (79) / SOC: 78 / Confidence: High (84) / Reasons: JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, IOC 39, detection evidence context, Japan source / Summary: AI開発ツールに興味のある人々を「claude.ai」のチャット共有画面や「GitLab Pages」の偽サイトに誘導し、不正なコマンドを実行させる「マルバタイジング＆ClickFix攻撃」を分析しました。

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Poses as Ransomware Gang to Mask Cyber Espionage
item.description: Source: Infosecurity Magazine / Source attributes: United Kingdom / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware, APT, Threat Actor / Topics: MuddyWater, Ransomware, Malware, APT / Priority: High (78) / SOC: 84 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: An NCC Group report warns state-backed hackers are attempting to hide activity by posing as ransomware groups and deploying commercially available malware

item.title: ESET takes part in Operation Endgame to disrupt Amadey and Stealc
item.description: Source: Eset Research / Source attributes: Slovakia / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware / Topics: Malware / Priority: High (78) / SOC: 83 / Confidence: High (84) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, IOC 24, target and impact context, indicator context / Summary: ESET researchers assisted in the global disruption of the Amadey botnet and Stealc infostealer, providing technical analysis, infrastructure tracking, and affiliate-level insights / Also reported by: Bleeping Computer, Security Affairs, HackRead

item.title: Cyber Risk Assumptions Are Becoming Obsolete Due to AI, Warn Five Eyes
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Identity, AI Security, Network, Detection, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Identity, AI Security / Priority: High (78) / SOC: 70 / Confidence: Medium (53) / Reasons: EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector / Summary: AI Cyber Risk is evolving faster than many organizations can adapt, prompting a joint warning from the Five Eyes cyber security agencies. The agencies have called on business leaders, executives, and boards to act now, warning that advances in artificial intelligence are rapidly transforming the cyber threat landscape and shortening the time available to respond to emerging risks. In a coordinated statement, the leaders of the Five Eyes cyber security partnership said that while AI has the potential to improve defensive capabilities, it is also accelerating the speed, scale, and sophistication

item.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Malware, Ransomware, Network / Topics: Microsoft, Ransomware, Malware / Priority: High (76) / SOC: 82 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, critical sector / Summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC. "The main common goal was to disrupt the 'assembly lines' cybercriminals use to launch ransomware, financial fraud, and attacks on critical infrastructure," Europol said in

item.title: CVE-2026-20253: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Splunk Enterprise RCE
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Vulnerability, Network / Topics: CVE-2026-20253, Vulnerability / Priority: High (75) / SOC: 63 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, IOC 9, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context / Summary: CVE-2026-20253: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Splunk Enterprise RCE Splunk Enterprise admins should prioritize patching CVE-2026-20253, a critical vulnerability that allows a network-reachable, unauthenticated attacker to create or truncate arbitrary files on the Splunk server. Under certain conditions, this can be chained into remote code execution (RCE), making exposure the main risk driver. CISA has […] / Also reported by: The HackersNews

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Installs ManageEngine Endpoint Central for Persistent Remote Access
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Malware, Endpoint / Topics: Malware / Priority: High (74) / SOC: 76 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, IOC 15, coherent attack event / Summary: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Installs ManageEngine Endpoint Central for Persistent Remote Access A newly reported malware campaign uses WhatsApp direct messages to deliver VBScript (VBS/VBE) attachments that look like routine business documents. If a recipient downloads and then opens the attachment in WhatsApp Desktop or WhatsApp Web, the script starts a staged infection chain that ends […]

item.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
item.description: Source: Bleeping Computer / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Security, Phishing, Data Breach, Network / Topics: Phishing, Data Breach / Priority: High (71) / SOC: 71 / Confidence: Medium (64) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector / Summary: Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...] / Primary source: ocrportal.hhs.gov https://ocrportal.hhs.gov/ocr/breach/breach_report_hip.jsf

item.title: Backdoor.Mistic: New Backdoor May be Linked to Ransomware Access Broker
item.description: Source: Security.com Threat Research / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Detection, Ransomware / Topics: Ransomware, Malware / Priority: High (70) / SOC: 73 / Confidence: High (78) / Reasons: EN attack activity, IOC 38, coherent attack event, uncertain reporting / Summary: Stealthy new backdoor used in cybercrime intrusions since April 2026 may be associated with Woodgnat (aka KongTuke), an initial access broker whose ModeloRAT toolkit has fed Qilin and other ransomware operations. / Also reported by: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Attributes Mastra AI Supply Chain Attack to North Korea
item.description: Source: Infosecurity Magazine / Source attributes: United Kingdom / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Threat Actor, Supply Chain, AI Security / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain, AI Security / Priority: High (69) / SOC: 69 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN Japan/Asia relevance, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan related / Summary: North Korean threat actor Sapphire Sleet has been linked to a supply chain attack targeting Mastra, according to Microsoft security researchers

item.title: Lost in relocation: analysis of a new loader distributing CASTLESTEALER
item.description: Source: Elastic / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Detection / Topics: Malware / Priority: High (68) / SOC: 71 / Confidence: High (76) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, detection evidence context / Summary: Find out how a new obfuscated loader evades static detection using .reloc section abuse, five anti-VM/language checks and MBA obfuscation to deliver infostealer malware via Google Ads.

item.title: サイバーリーズンが見た攻撃 第4回 EDRの無効化を狙う「タンパリング」とは？ ——タンパリングに打ち勝つ製品能力と「防いで終わり」にしない真のセキュリティ
item.description: Source: Cybereason / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Endpoint / Topics: Ransomware / Priority: High (68) / SOC: 69 / Confidence: High (83) / Reasons: JA attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context / Summary: プロローグ：攻撃者が最初に行う「監視カメラの破壊」 サイバー攻撃者が組織のネットワークに深く侵入した際、彼らが最も恐れるのは「防御側の目」、すなわちEDRなどのセキュリティセンサーです。熟練した攻撃者は、被害者組織に侵入ができても結果を急ぎ過ぎず、入念にセンサーの無効化（タンパリング）やアンインストールを試みます。つまり、EDRの能力を攻撃者こそが良く理解しているのです。 シリーズ第4回となる今回は、ある企業を標的としたランサムウェア攻撃において、攻撃者が仕掛けた執拗な「セキュリティ製品の無効化」に対して、Cybereason EDRがいかにしてその攻撃を打ち破ったのか。そして、その事象を単な […続きを読む]

item.title: Amadey の栄枯盛衰: C2 サーバの長期的な観測から得られた知見
item.description: Source: 三井物産セキュアディレクション / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel / Priority: High (67) / SOC: 67 / Confidence: Medium (68) / Reasons: EN attack activity, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, indicator context, Japan source
item.source: 三井物産セキュアディレクション

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Priority: High (65) / SOC: 57 / Confidence: Medium (64) / Reasons: EN Japan/Asia relevance, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, Japan related / Summary: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software. Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and WhatsApp Web across Malaysia, Brazil, India, Mexico, Singapore, the U.K., Spain, Taiwan, Australia, / Primary source: Securelist https://securelist.com/whatsapp-vbs-rmm-campaign/120290/

item.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Ransomware, Endpoint, Detection / Topics: Ransomware / Priority: High (64) / SOC: 74 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context / Summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing system defenses before deploying the encryptor. This mature portfolio of EDR-terminating tools is centered around a framework that's known as GentleKiller. "They also incorporate third-party or

item.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Identity / Topics: Fortinet, Identity / Priority: High (64) / SOC: 66 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector / Summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential lists, searching for exposed services, brute-forcing accessible systems, and deploying bespoke

item.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Exploit, Threat Actor / Topics: Cisco, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: High (64) / SOC: 60 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME). The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230 (CVSS score: 8.6), is a case of improper input validation for specific HTTP requests that could allow an unauthenticated, remote / Also reported by: Security Affairs

item.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Malware, AI Security, Endpoint / Topics: Apple/iOS, Malware, AI Security / Priority: High (62) / SOC: 67 / Confidence: Medium (64) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, indicator context / Summary: A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer has been found to embed a prompt injection payload designed to trick a malware analyst's artificial intelligence (AI) tools and trick it into aborting or refusing an analysis of the artifact. The malware has been codenamed Gaslight owing to this deceptive behavior. It's been assessed with high confidence that the tool is / Also reported by: Infosecurity Magazine / Primary source: SentinelOne https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/macos-gaslight-rust-backdoor-turns-prompt-injection-on-the-analyst-not-the-sandbox/

item.title: A closer look at Africa’s evolving cyberthreat landscape
item.description: Source: Barracuda Labs / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel / Priority: High (61) / SOC: 61 / Confidence: High (77) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, detection evidence context, indicator context / Summary: Cyber threats in Africa are evolving, with recent data showing a shift from disruption-heavy attacks toward more complex, multi-stage activity. Compared to North America and Europe, Africa’s cyber threat landscape is still more visibility-driven, shaped by public disruption and uneven reporting practices. For defenders and MSPs, this shift highlights the need to prepare for hybrid attacks and monitor early indicators of compromise.

item.title: Amadey's Rise and Fall: What Six and a Half Years of C2 Observation Reveal

item.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Malware, Threat Actor, Supply Chain / Topics: Supply Chain, Malware / Priority: High (60) / SOC: 68 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event / Summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code. "Attackers compromised the vendor's build and distribution pipeline, injecting backdoor code into Pro plugin releases distributed through official licensed update channels," Wordfence said in an analysis

item.title: Malicious Edge extension abuses Native Messaging as bridge to malware

item.title: Klue Breach: What You Need to Know
item.description: Source: SOCRadar Blog / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, IOC, Identity / Topics: Identity / Priority: Medium (58) / SOC: 55 / Confidence: High (82) / Reasons: EN attack activity, IOC 9, target and impact context / Summary: Klue Breach: What You Need to Know The Klue breach shows how stolen OAuth tokens from a trusted SaaS integration can expose Salesforce CRM data. Learn what happened, which companies confirmed impact, what data was exposed, and how defenders should respond. What Happened in the Klue Breach? The Klue breach was a third-party Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) […]

item.title: Millenium: A RAT Rewritten, A Threat Multiplied
item.description: Source: Group-IB / Source attributes: Singapore / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Exploit, Malware, Threat Actor, Application Security / Topics: Exploit, Malware / Priority: Medium (58) / SOC: 54 / Confidence: High (88) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event / Summary: Group-IB analyzes Millenium RAT version 4.*, a remote access trojan that has undergone an architectural shift from .NET to native C++, while continuing to leverage the Telegram Bot API for command and control, requiring no dedicated server infrastructure. This blog also profiles the developer “ShinyEnigma”, and threat actor cluster “Y2K Operators” responsible for active Millenium RAT exploitation campaigns. Over 62,000 compromised endpoints across more than 160 countries have been identified, with infections accelerating sharply in Q1 2026.

item.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Malware / Topics: Malware / Priority: Medium (57) / SOC: 60 / Confidence: Medium (64) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors since April 2026. According to Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team, the backdoor, also tracked as MLTBackdoor, is said to be linked to an initial access broker (IAB) named / Primary source: Security.com Threat Research https://www.security.com/threat-intelligence/new-mistic-backdoor-modeloRAT

item.title: StrikeShark: investigating a new campaign delivering Cobalt Strike through SharkLoader

item.title: Compromised GitHub action codfish/semantic-release-action steals CI/CD secrets
item.description: Source: Aikido Security Research / Source attributes: Belgium / Research / Primary / Categories: Supply Chain, Application Security, Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Identity / Topics: Supply Chain, Vulnerability, Identity / Priority: Medium (57) / SOC: 56 / Confidence: High (76) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context / Summary: codfish/semantic-release-action was compromised on June 24, 2026. Attackers repointed v2–v5 tags to a Miasma credential-stealing payload targeting CI/CD secrets. Here's what happened and how to check if you're affected. Category: Vulnerabilities & Threats

item.title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
item.description: Source: SecurityWeek / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware / Topics: Microsoft, Malware / Priority: Medium (56) / SOC: 63 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event, indicator context / Summary: Hundreds of C&C servers were disrupted in an operation involving law enforcement and several cybersecurity companies. The post Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: 正常な履歴書のように見えるが、実行した瞬間に感染開始
item.description: Source: AhnLab / Source attributes: South Korea / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Phishing, Threat Actor / Topics: Malware, Phishing / Priority: Medium (56) / SOC: 56 / Confidence: High (73) / Reasons: JA attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context, uncertain reporting / Summary: 最近、履歴書ファイルを装った悪意のあるショートカット (LNK) ファイルが拡散されており、企業ユーザーの注意が求められている。攻撃者は企業名や職種名を含む履歴書文書のようなファイル名を使用し、実行時には正常なデコイ (Decoy) 文書を表示することで、ユーザーの警戒心を低下させる。その後、追加の悪意のあるファイルをダウンロードしてバックドアマルウェアの実行を試みるほか、タスクスケジューラへの登録、スタートアップへの登録、DLL Side Loading などによって持続性を確保する。 特に、履歴書を装った文書ファイルは業務環境で容易に開かれる可能性があるため、セキュリティ担当者および従業員には慎重な確認が求められる。 本ブログでは、履歴書ファイルを装って拡散されている悪意のある LNK ファイルの主な特徴と対策について解説する。...

item.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Ransomware, Phishing, AI Security / Topics: Ransomware, Phishing, AI Security / Priority: Medium (56) / SOC: 51 / Confidence: Low (42) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN Japan/Asia relevance, Japan related / Summary: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized criminal networks, and a disparity in cybersecurity maturity. According to INTERPOL's 2025/2026 Asia and South Pacific Cyberthreat Assessment Report, phishing has emerged as the most widespread and

item.title: Malicious hackers exploit Cisco zero-day for highest access level at communications service provider
item.description: Source: CyberScoop / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Research, Threat Intel, Exploit, Zero-Day / Topics: Cisco, Network Edge, Exploit, Zero-Day / Priority: Medium (55) / SOC: 48 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: Mandiant detailed the incident in a blog post Wednesday, but it’s unclear who was behind it or if they managed to get broad visibility into the victim’s internal traffic. item.title: 急増する金融犯罪の手口をFC3マトリクスで読み解く（3）～「口座乗っ取り（証券）」編
item.description: Source: LAC / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Priority: Medium (55) / SOC: 40 / Confidence: High (72) / Reasons: JA attack activity, JA critical sector, Japan source, critical sector / Summary: 個人の証券口座の認証情報を詐取し、乗っ取った口座で一斉に株の売買を行い、株価の操作とそれに伴う利益を得る手口を、FC3マトリクスで体系的に捉え、対策の検討にどのように活用できるのかについて、事例を交えながら攻撃の典型パターンと対策の勘所を解説します。

The two flaws added to the catalog are: The first flaw, tracked […]

item.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
item.description: Source: Vulners / Source attributes: Global / Vulnerability Database / Aggregator / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Malware / Topics: Vulnerability, Malware / Priority: Medium (53) / SOC: 66 / Confidence: Low (25) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, uncertain reporting / Summary: An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code. According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube ID: cmedhionkhpnakcndndgjdbohmhepckk, has more than 10 million installs and carries a Featured badge on the Chrome Web Store. The extension description states that it allows users to prevent web page elements like ads, including preroll ads, from being displayed on the video sharing platform, as well as on external sites that load YouTube. While the add-on offers the promised functionality, it also fe

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer

According to Elastic Security Labs, the campaign leverages malicious Google Ads as a starting point to distribute the malware. Evidence indicates that the threat actor is likely Russian-speaking and financially motivated, owing to the

item.title: Hackers steal passport and driver’s license data of 3 million Texans

item.title: Nearly 15,000 infected websites cleaned in SocGholish crackdown
item.description: Source: Malwarebytes / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Network / Topics: Malware / Priority: Medium (52) / SOC: 49 / Confidence: High (78) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event / Summary: Thousands of everyday websites were cleaned as part of a global operation targeting the malware network behind fake browser update scams.

item.title: Security Is No Longer an IT Problem: Why Boards Must Rethink Cyber Resilience in the Age of AI
item.description: Source: Cofense / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Phishing, AI Security, Detection / Topics: Microsoft, Phishing, AI Security / Priority: Medium (52) / SOC: 48 / Confidence: High (77) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, detection evidence context / Summary: Today’s attackers are using AI to create polymorphic phishing campaigns that continuously evolve to evade traditional detection systems. They rotate URLs, vary sender identities, change subject lines, and modify content at scale. The result is that many organisations are discovering that even sophisticated email security tools and Microsoft 365 protections cannot stand alone against modern threats.

item.title: 🏴‍☠️ Aurora has just published a new victim : Corporación Primax S.A.
item.description: Source: ransomware.live / Source attributes: France / Research / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intel, Identity, ICS/OT, Network / Topics: Operational Technology, Ransomware, Identity, ICS/OT / Priority: Medium (52) / SOC: 43 / Confidence: Medium (67) / Reasons: EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN critical sector, critical sector / Summary: [distribution, fuel] ***.A. is Peru's largest fuel distribution company, operating 2,185+ stations across Peru, Ecuador, Colombia, and Uruguay with annualised revenue of approximately USD 3.4 billion (Peru alone). The dataset spans every function of the business: Complete financial reporting — Monthly P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, and EBITDA through May 2025. GRIO (Grupo Romero Investment Office) management reporting packages. Budget 2025 vs. actuals. Employee identity data for 15,000–60,000 individuals — DNI national ID numbers, bank accounts, salary amounts, pension fund details, scanned id

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Supply Chain / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain / Priority: Medium (49) / SOC: 49 / Confidence: Medium (64) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains. The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositories at dozens of the largest organizations worldwide, including Microsoft, Google, Apache, and / Primary source: novee.security https://novee.security/blog/cordyceps/

item.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Malware, DDoS, Network / Topics: Malware / Priority: Medium (48) / SOC: 52 / Confidence: Medium (48) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event / Summary: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. QiAnXin's XLab calls it AryStinger and counts at least 4,300 infected routers, a total it says is still rising. The distinction matters. AryStinger exists for the stage of an attack that comes before the break-in. Infected

item.title: Azure AD Graph Activity Logs: Ingestion and threat detection to close the visibility gap
item.description: Source: Elastic / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Cloud, Detection / Topics: Cloud Security, Cloud / Priority: Medium (48) / SOC: 47 / Confidence: Medium (65) / Reasons: EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN detection/analysis, detection evidence context / Summary: Azure AD Graph Activity Logs land in Elastic with full ECS parsing. Detect ROADrecon and AADInternals enumeration with ready-to-use detection rules.

item.title: Next-Gen Phishing Tactics Users Aren’t Ready For | Huntress
item.description: Source: huntress / Source attributes: Global / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Phishing, Identity / Topics: Phishing, Identity / Priority: Medium (47) / SOC: 43 / Confidence: High (72) / Reasons: EN attack activity, detection evidence context / Summary: Move past basic credential harvesting. Discover how modern attackers use ClickFix, BitB, and OAuth consent phishing—and how to train your users with Huntress SAT.

item.title: EvilTokens: How “Ghost” Code Threatens US and European Businesses
item.description: Source: ANY.RUN / Source attributes: United Arab Emirates / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Security, Threat Intel / Topics: Microsoft, Malware / Priority: Medium (46) / SOC: 39 / Confidence: High (82) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis / Summary: EvilTokens can hide serious account takeover risk from your SOC through “ghost” code that appears only after browser-side decryption. As a result, static URL analysis may miss the most important part of the attack, leaving teams with incomplete evidence, slower triage, and longer exposure to a potential Microsoft 365 compromise. Full browser-level inspection closes this gap by revealing […] The post EvilTokens: How “Ghost” Code Threatens US and European Businesses appeared first on ANY.RUN's Cybersecurity Blog .

item.description: Source: Cybersecurity Dive / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel / Topics: Fortinet / Priority: Medium (45) / SOC: 49 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event, target and impact context / Summary: Security researchers warn of a months-long FortiBleed campaign targeting western organizations.

item.title: Stealthy new backdoor surfaces in attacks on multiple sectors
item.description: Source: Help Net Security / Source attributes: Croatia / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware / Topics: Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Medium (45) / SOC: 48 / Confidence: Medium (47) / Reasons: EN attack activity, target and impact context / Summary: A relatively new backdoor called Mistic has been deployed in multiple attacks since April 2026 targeting organizations in the insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors, according to Symantec. The malware appears to be associated with Woodgnat, also known as KongTuke, a financially motivated initial access broker (IAB) active since at least May 2024 that has been connected to ransomware operations including Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta. “Woodgnat reportedly functions primarily as … More → The post Stealthy new backdoor surfaces in attacks on multipl

item.title: Linux Process Name Masquerading, (Wed, Jun 24th)
item.description: Source: SANS ISC / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Endpoint, Application Security / Topics: Linux, Malware / Priority: Medium (45) / SOC: 40 / Confidence: High (75) / Reasons: EN attack activity, indicator context / Summary: In a previous diary, I talked about stack strings&#;x26;#;x5b;1&#;x26;#;x5d; with a practical example of them. Since my SEC670 class, I&#;x26;#;xe2;&#;x26;#;x80;&#;x26;#;x99;m even more interested&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;in malware obfuscation techniques. I had&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;a look at process names. When you list running processes on a computer, can you trust what you see&#;x26;#;x3f; If you&#;x26;#;39;re facing a rootkit, malicious processes can be simply hidden (the API calls or commands to list processed have been tampered). But a malicious process&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;can a

item.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
item.description: Source: Schneier on Security / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: AI Security, Threat Intel, Malware / Topics: AI Security, Malware / Priority: Medium (45) / SOC: 39 / Confidence: High (75) / Reasons: EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis / Summary: At least one malware developer is adding text about nuclear and biological weapons to their spyware, in an effort to stop automatic AI analysis. Details : The _index.js payload begins with a large JavaScript block comment containing fake system instructions and policy-triggering content. Because it is inside a comment, it does not affect JavaScript execution. The runtime skips it. The real malware begins after the comment with a try{eval(…)} wrapper around a large character-code array and a ROT-style substitution function. This header appears designed for AI-mediated analysis, not for Node, Bu

item.title: Hacker gets 18 months for attack that compromised 60,000 betting accounts
item.description: Source: Help Net Security / Source attributes: Croatia / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Identity, Incident Response / Topics: Identity / Priority: Medium (43) / SOC: 45 / Confidence: Medium (47) / Reasons: EN attack activity, target and impact context / Summary: A 21-year-old man known online as “Snoopy” was sentenced to 18 months in prison for his role in a scheme that hacked user accounts on a fantasy sports and betting website and sold access to them, causing hundreds of thousands of dollars in losses. Nathan Austad of Farmington, Minnesota, pleaded guilty in December 2025 to one count of conspiracy to commit computer intrusion. According to prosecutors, Austad and his co-conspirators launched a credential stuffing attack … More → The post Hacker gets 18 months for attack that compromised 60,000 betting accounts appeared first on Help Net Security

item.title: New GhostShell Hacking Group Targets Ukraine’s Drone Defense Sector
item.description: Source: HackRead / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Security, Threat Intel / Priority: Medium (42) / SOC: 37 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN critical sector, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector / Summary: Researchers warn GhostShell is using fake drone documents to target Ukrainian defence teams, stealing passwords and sensitive data in a new cyber campaign.

item.title: Klue Breach Enables Hackers to Compromise Cybersecurity Firms via OAuth Tokens
item.description: Source: Infosecurity Magazine / Source attributes: United Kingdom / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Identity / Topics: Identity / Priority: Medium (41) / SOC: 44 / Confidence: Low (43) / Reasons: EN attack activity, coherent attack event / Summary: At least four cybersecurity firms confirmed they have been affected by a breach of business intelligence platform Klue via Salesforce integration

item.title: In a first, a court takedown goes after two cybercrime tools at once
item.description: Source: CyberScoop / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: AI Security, Threat Intel, Malware / Topics: Microsoft, AI Security, Malware / Priority: Low (37) / SOC: 38 / Confidence: Low (42) / Reasons: EN attack activity, indicator context / Summary: Microsoft, with law enforcement and industry partners, disrupted more than 200 command and control servers for Amadey and StealC, often used in conjunction. The post In a first, a court takedown goes after two cybercrime tools at once appeared first on CyberScoop .
